content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Toll Free: 1 800-424-9100 • Conflict of Interest
916 322-5660
322-5901
322-6441
322-6444

February 13, 1979

Robert L. Leslie                                                A-79-092
418 Calaveras Way
Sonora, CA 95370

Dear Mr. Leslie:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm in writing the
advice given to you by telephone in response to your letter
dated January 20, 1979.

     The facts, as presented by your letter, were that a
decision is currently pending by the City of Sonora whether to
rezone property which is contiguous to your personal residence.
Assuming you are disqualified from participating in the rezoning
decisions affecting this parcel of property, you have asked
the following series of questions: May you participate in the
rezoning decisions in your private capacity; if there is a tie
vote, may you cast the deciding vote; and finally to what degree
of accountability will you be held by relying on advice rendered
by the city attorney which was later found to be in error.

     With respect to your first question, I pointed out during
our telephone conversations of February 2 and 9, 1979, that you
may participate in the rezoning decision in your private capacity
pursuant to Commission Regulation 18700(d)(2). An official is
prohibited by the Political Reform Act from participating in a
governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest
provided, however that,

     Appearances by a public official as a member of the
     general public before an agency in the course of its
     prescribed governmental function to represent himself
     or herself on matters related solely to his or her
     personal interest.

                              2 Cal. Adm. Code 18700(d)(2)

are permitted. You indicated during our conversation that you

Robert L. Lustic                                                    Page Two
February 13, 1979


might become spokesperson for a group of neighbors in the
affected area.  I informed you that such activity generally
would be contrary to the requirement of the Regulation since
you would not be representing solely your own interests.  If
you wish to pursue this question further, you should outline
in writing the specific governmental decision involved, the
nature of your personal interest and the nature of the group
you wish to represent.  Based on this information we will
provide you with additional advice.

Government Code Section 87101 of the Act addresses your
second question about casting tie-breaking votes.  The Act
is quite specific in that an official who was previously
disqualified cannot return for the purposes of casting a
tie-breaking vote.  Section 87101 provides,

Section 87100 [the disqualification provision]
does not prevent any public official from making
or participating in the making of a governmental
decision to the extent his participation is
legally required for the action or decision to
be made.  The fact that an official's vote is
needed to break a tie does not make his partici-
pation legally required for purposes of this
section.
                                        (Emphasis added.)

Therefore, you may not participate in the rezoning decision for
the purpose of breaking a vote deadlock.

Finally, you ask what liabilities may be incurred by
relying on advice given by the city attorney which is later
found to be incorrect.  I indicated that if the advice received
from the city attorney was sought and complied with in good faith
our office would not take any enforcement action against you.
As a practical matter, it would be unlikely that any other person
would succeed in an enforcement action under these circumstances.
However, nothing in the law would bar the initial filing of such
a suit.

I hope that our advice has been helpful.  If we can be of
further assistance regarding this matter, please do not hesitate
to contact our office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Local Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:nrw
